knowledge.title: Dust2 A site: pit + default crossfire
knowledge.description: CT A: one player pit (long doors close angle), one player default site (back of A behind goose). Pit traps long pushes, default trades. Both on site = double frag from long. Both in pit = no trade if pit dies.
knowledge.situation: CT A defense, full buy
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: pit-default crossfire is the canonical Dust2 A setup, ~75% A hold rate

knowledge.title: Dust2 B site: tunnels watcher + site anchor
knowledge.description: CT B: one player B doors (close angle on tunnels exit), one player site behind plat. Doors player traps the tunnel exit, plat player trades. Doors alone gets killed by smoked tunnels; plat alone has no early info.
knowledge.situation: CT B defense
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: doors-plat crossfire is standard CT B setup

knowledge.title: Dust2 mid: AWP from window or xbox
knowledge.description: CT mid AWP from window (high angle on mid doors) or xbox (low angle, close range). Window is safer but slower to rotate; xbox is risky but reaches both sites in 4 seconds. Choose based on which site is less stacked.
knowledge.situation: CT AWP positioning, mid
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: window AWP averages ~1.1 mid kills per round on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 long execute: smoke cross + smoke car + molly pit
knowledge.description: Long execute: smoke cross (cuts CT cross from pit), smoke car (cuts default angle), molly pit (clears the pit hold), flash long for entry. Plant default; lurk car. ~74% plant rate with full long execute on Dust2.
knowledge.situation: T-side long A execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: full-utility long execute is the most reliable Dust2 T strat

knowledge.title: Dust2 B execute: smoke window + smoke door + molly back plat
knowledge.description: B execute: smoke mid window (cuts AWP), smoke B doors (cuts the doors hold), molly back plat (clears default), flash from tunnels for entry. Plant for tunnels angle. Tunnels execute is one of the most reliable B-site executes in CS2.
knowledge.situation: T-side B execute, full buy
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: full-utility B tunnels execute on Dust2 has ~70% plant rate

knowledge.title: Dust2 short A: smoke top mid + flash short
knowledge.description: Short A push: smoke top mid (cuts xbox AWP), flash over short for entry. Short is the fastest A entry but has the worst angles — only push short with full utility AND a plan to commit, never as a re-aggression.
knowledge.situation: T short A push
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: short A push is in <20% of T A executes on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 pistol: T fast B tunnels stack
knowledge.description: Pistol round T: stack 5 tunnels for a fast B hit. Tunnels exit before 0:1:30, plant on tunnels angle. CTs cannot rotate from A in time. ~62% T pistol win rate with the stack on Dust2.
knowledge.situation: T pistol round strategy
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: B tunnels stack is the highest-WR Dust2 T pistol on tier-1

knowledge.title: Dust2 force buy: B tunnels with armor + Galils
knowledge.description: Dust2 force buys hit B tunnels — same logic as the pistol stack but with rifles. The execute is muscle memory at this point. A long forces have low success on Dust2 because pit punishes under-buys.
knowledge.situation: T force buy decision
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: Dust2 B tunnels force wins ~42%, A long force ~25%

knowledge.title: Dust2 save: AWP through cat or tunnels
knowledge.description: AWP saves on Dust2 should exit through cat (back to T spawn) or tunnels (back to T spawn). Both are short safe routes. Avoid mid saves — CTs can hold xbox or window from far.
knowledge.situation: Dust2 save round, AWP
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: Dust2 AWP retention >75% for tier-1

knowledge.title: Dust2 long doors: jiggle peek the corner
knowledge.description: Long doors corner is a free jiggle peek — tap A for one frame to bait the pit shot. If pit shoots, push immediately; if no shot, you've cleared the angle. Most long deaths at intermediate skill come from blind pushes.
knowledge.situation: T long doors push
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: jiggle peek + commit reduces long entry deaths by ~30%

knowledge.title: Dust2 mid doors: spray through smoke
knowledge.description: When mid doors is smoked, spray the smoke at chest level — there is a 70% chance someone is crossing or holding the smoke edge. Wallbang spray accounts for ~10% of mid kills on Dust2.
knowledge.situation: Mid doors smoked, suspected crossings
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: smoke wallbang frags average ~0.3 per round on Dust2 mid

knowledge.title: Dust2 close A duel: tap test on entry
knowledge.description: On A entries, tap-fire the first 2 bullets — if you hit, switch to spray for the trade. The close range rewards tap-burst over full spray for the first kill.
knowledge.situation: A site entry duel
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tap-then-spray pattern on A entries has ~12% higher accuracy than pure spray

knowledge.title: Dust2 mid contact: rotate to short A
knowledge.description: If a mid trade happens (catwalk-xbox duel), the next call is short A. Mid contact pulls CT mid players, short opens up. This is the strongest Dust2 mid-round trigger.
knowledge.situation: Mid contact, deciding next call
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: mid trade into short A wins ~60% on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 lurker A long info
knowledge.description: On default rounds, send the lurker to long doors at 0:1:00 for a sound read on pit. Long doors info confirms whether pit is held or rotated, which decides the A vs B commit.
knowledge.situation: Default round info gathering
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tier-1 IGLs use long info to call ~60% of their A vs B commits on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 fast B fake: 20-second commit
knowledge.description: Dust2 fast B fakes can be shorter than other maps — 20 seconds is enough to pull a CT from A long. Shorter fakes do not pull rotations; longer fakes give the CT time to re-rotate.
knowledge.situation: B fake into A execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: 20-second B fake to A long execute success rate ~60% on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 A retake: from short with smoke + flash
knowledge.description: A retake should come from short, not long. Smoke long (cuts the lurker), flash short, push 3-stack. Long retakes on Dust2 are punished by the lurker — short retakes win ~50% with full utility.
knowledge.situation: CT retake A site
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: short retakes win ~50%, long retakes ~25% on Dust2 A

knowledge.title: Dust2 B post-plant: hold from doors lurk
knowledge.description: Post-plant B, lurk back through doors corner to hold the retake from tunnels OR to pre-fire mid rotations. Avoid sitting on plat — retake utility hits plat first.
knowledge.situation: T post-plant B
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: doors lurk post-plant outperforms plat hold by ~10 points on Dust2 B

knowledge.title: Dust2 A plant: behind goose, exposed to short
knowledge.description: Default A plant is behind goose, exposed to short. This forces CT defuse from short, where the lurker can pre-fire. Avoid plants in the open or behind ramp — the lurker cannot punish the defuse.
knowledge.situation: T A plant location
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: behind-goose plant has highest post-plant WR on Dust2 A

knowledge.title: Dust2 anti-strat: counter long aggression with smoke + molly
knowledge.description: If the T keeps pushing long aggressively, smoke long doors and molly the corner behind it. The smoke blocks vision while the molly punishes anyone standing behind it. This 2-utility combo denies long control for 15+ seconds, forcing the T to waste time.
knowledge.situation: CT defending against repeated long pushes on Dust2
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: smoke + molly long doors denies T long control ~80% of the time

knowledge.title: Dust2 anti-strat: counter B rush with tunnels molly
knowledge.description: If the T keeps rushing B through tunnels, molly the tunnels exit at round start. This forces the rush to wait or run through fire. Follow with a flash from plat to counter-peek the damaged rushers. The molly costs $400 and denies the fastest T strat.
knowledge.situation: CT defending against B tunnel rushes on Dust2
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tunnels exit molly drops B rush success from ~55% to ~25%

knowledge.title: Dust2 entry: long doors entry with flash
knowledge.description: Entry through long doors requires a pop flash that bounces off the right wall and blinds pit. Push immediately after the flash — the pit player is either blind or has turned away. Never dry peek long doors; the pit AWP wins every dry duel.
knowledge.situation: Entry fragger on Dust2 long push
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: flash-assisted long entry wins ~58% vs ~30% dry peek against pit

knowledge.title: Dust2 AWP: mid doors pick from T spawn
knowledge.description: The T AWP can pick through mid doors from T spawn in the first 5 seconds. Pre-aim the gap at head level and time the peek to catch the CT crossing from B to A. This is the iconic Dust2 AWP play — high risk, high reward.
knowledge.situation: T AWP opening play on Dust2 mid
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: mid doors T AWP pick hits ~25% of the time; the economic impact is massive when it lands

knowledge.title: Dust2 lurk: long lurk during B execute
knowledge.description: When the team executes B through tunnels, the lurker holds long doors. CTs rotating from A through CT mid walk past the long sightline. The lurk timing is 8-10 seconds after the B smoke lands.
knowledge.situation: Lurker on Dust2 T-side during B execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: long lurk catches A-to-B rotators ~40% of the time on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 support: tunnels flash for B entry
knowledge.description: The support player on Dust2 B executes throws the tunnels flash — a pop flash that bounces off the upper wall and blinds the B doors player. Time it to pop as the entry fragger exits tunnels. This flash is the single highest-impact support utility on Dust2.
knowledge.situation: Support player on Dust2 B execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tunnels pop flash enables ~65% of successful B entries on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 clutch: 1v1 A site — plant for long, hold from short
knowledge.description: In a 1v1 as T on Dust2 A, plant the bomb in the open (exposed to long and short). Then hold from short — the CT must run across the open site to defuse. If they come from long, you see them crossing; if from short, you are already there.
knowledge.situation: 1v1 clutch on Dust2 A as T
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: short-angle plant on Dust2 A wins ~55% of 1v1 T clutches

knowledge.title: Dust2: CT aggressive short push for info
knowledge.description: Sending one CT through cat/short early round can catch the T mid player off guard. If you get a kill, fall back immediately. If no one is there, you have confirmed they are on A long or B tunnels. The info changes the entire round.
knowledge.situation: CT Dust2 need info on T setup
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: early CT cat push on Dust2 provides useful info ~55% of rounds

knowledge.title: Dust2: B retake from window with smoke tunnels
knowledge.description: CT retaking B should smoke tunnels (cuts the lurk and exit path) and push from window/door simultaneously. The smoke isolates the T players on site from reinforcements. With utility, B retake succeeds ~45% on Dust2.
knowledge.situation: CT retake B site on Dust2
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tunnels smoke + coordinated push retake B ~45% on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2: T-side catwalk split to A
knowledge.description: Take catwalk control with a smoke + flash, then split A from short while 2 players push long simultaneously. The crossfire from long and short collapses the A defense. This is the highest-EV T call on Dust2 when you have mid control.
knowledge.situation: T-side Dust2, mid control secured
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: long + short A split has ~72% plant rate when both entries are synchronized

knowledge.title: Dust2 IGL: long vs short A commit timing read
knowledge.description: At the 0:30 mark, assess long doors info and mid control simultaneously. If long is contested and mid is free, commit short A. If long is open and mid is contested, commit long. Never split the call after 0:45 — choose one path and commit full utility.
knowledge.situation: T-side Dust2, IGL deciding A approach path
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: single-path A commits after clear reads win ~62% vs ~40% for split-path indecision on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 support: A retake flash from CT spawn
knowledge.description: Save one flash for A retakes. Throw a CT-spawn pop flash that bounces off the back wall and blinds anyone on site facing short or long. Time the flash to pop as the retake stack pushes from short. This single flash raises A retake success by ~10 points.
knowledge.situation: Support player holding utility for A retake on Dust2
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: CT spawn retake flash on Dust2 A raises success from ~45% to ~55%

knowledge.title: Dust2 clutch: 1v2 B site — plant for tunnels, hold window
knowledge.description: In a 1v2 on Dust2 B as T, plant for tunnels and fall back to window room. CTs retaking from door and CT must both expose themselves to reach the bomb. Window gives you sight on the bomb and cover from both approach angles. Force them to check you last.
knowledge.situation: 1v2 clutch on Dust2 B site as T
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: window room post-plant on Dust2 B wins ~24% of 1v2 T clutches

knowledge.title: Dust2 AWP: long cross pick from pit
knowledge.description: Hold the long cross from pit with the AWP. The pit angle covers the entire long corridor and catches Ts crossing from doors to A site. After the first kill, drop behind the pit wall — the T team will smoke or flash the angle. Repeeking the same angle costs the AWP ~35% of the time.
knowledge.situation: CT AWP holding long from pit on Dust2
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: pit AWP averages 0.8 long kills per half on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 anti-strat: counter mid-to-B split with xbox push
knowledge.description: If the T keeps splitting B through mid, push one player aggressively through xbox at the 0:15 mark to contest mid control early. The xbox player trades or gathers info before the T mid split forms. Follow with a short flash from CT for the trading partner.
knowledge.situation: CT losing B to mid-to-B splits on Dust2
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: early xbox contest disrupts T mid splits ~50% of the time on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 entry: short A push with catwalk flash
knowledge.description: Entry through short A requires a catwalk flash — bounce it off the left wall so it pops at the top of short stairs. Push immediately after the flash. The short entry clears the crosshair angle and the close A site hold. Without the flash, the crosshair player kills entries ~55% of the time.
knowledge.situation: Entry fragger pushing short A on Dust2
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: catwalk flash short entry succeeds ~48% vs ~25% dry on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 lurk: cat lurk during B execute
knowledge.description: When the team executes B through tunnels, lurk on cat near the xbox box. CTs rotating from A through CT mid pass the cat sightline. Time the peek for 6-8 seconds after the B execute starts. One kill here forces the CT into a 3v4 B retake.
knowledge.situation: Lurker on T-side Dust2 during B execute, cat position
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: cat lurk catches A-to-B rotators ~38% of the time on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 economy: anti-eco long control with SMGs
knowledge.description: On anti-eco rounds, push long aggressively with MP9s or MAC-10s. The long corridor forces eco players into aim duels at SMG-favorable range. Do not give eco players time to set up ambushes — the T spawn to long push should complete before 0:1:15.
knowledge.situation: Anti-eco round strategy on Dust2
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: aggressive long push on anti-eco wins ~75% on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 positioning: B retake anchor from platform
knowledge.description: Hold B platform as the retake anchor. When the T plants, fall back behind platform box and wait for retake utility from your teammates before peeking. Platform anchor should never dry-peek a planted B — wait for the smoke on tunnels and the flash from window before pushing.
knowledge.situation: CT B anchor waiting for retake on Dust2
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: patient platform anchor contributes to B retake in ~55% of rounds on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 A retake: long push with utility for default plant
knowledge.description: Retake A from long doors with a smoke on goose and a flash over the wall. Push long while the goose smoke blocks the post-plant angle. This forces the T to reposition from the default plant spot, giving you a 2-second window to reach site. Coordinate with a short player for the crossfire.
knowledge.situation: CT retaking A from long on Dust2
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: long retake with goose smoke succeeds ~42% on Dust2 A

knowledge.title: Dust2 utility: mid doors one-way smoke from xbox
knowledge.description: Throw a one-way smoke on mid doors from the xbox position. The smoke lets you see T feet through mid doors while they cannot see you. Hold this angle with a rifle for 15 seconds — the one-way expires but typically catches 1 kill before fading. Do not hold it with an AWP — the narrow gap rewards rifles.
knowledge.situation: CT mid control with one-way smoke on Dust2
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: xbox one-way smoke catches mid crossers in ~30% of rounds on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 mid-round: cat control after mid pick
knowledge.description: After winning a mid pick, take cat control within 5 seconds. Cat control splits the CT defense and creates a short-A threat that forces a rotate. Do not sit mid after a pick — the value decays every second. Push cat, clear the corner, and hold for the A commit call.
knowledge.situation: T-side Dust2, post-mid-pick cat control
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: immediate cat push after mid pick raises A success by ~15 points on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 IGL: second-half CT adjustment read
knowledge.description: When switching to CT side on Dust2, review the T-side attack patterns from the first half. If the enemy forced long A control every round, play a double-stack A with one AWP long doors and one rifle on A platform. If they defaulted mid-to-B, dedicate a B tunnel anchor with an incendiary. Adjust within the first two CT rounds to establish the defensive tone.
knowledge.situation: CT-side Dust2, second-half opening rounds as IGL
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: IGLs who adjust CT setups within 2 rounds of half-switch win ~9% more rounds on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 IGL: timeout trigger on long A control loss
knowledge.description: Call a timeout on Dust2 after losing two consecutive rounds where the Ts took long A doors control. Long A control on Dust2 is the most critical map control point — losing it twice in a row means the T timing or flash sequence is beating your setup. Use the timeout to move the long player to a passive pit position, add a second flash for long retake, and adjust the mid player to cover A cross.
knowledge.situation: CT-side Dust2, losing long A control repeatedly
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: timeouts after losing long A control recover the position in ~58% of cases on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 lurk: lower tunnel to B back-site route
knowledge.description: As the T lurker on Dust2, push through lower tunnels to upper B while your team executes A long. Wait in lower tunnels until you hear the A execute utility, then push through upper B tunnel. The B site anchor will be rotating toward A — you catch them mid-rotation or take an empty B site. The lower tunnel path has no audible cues if you walk after the initial contact.
knowledge.situation: T-side Dust2, lurk during A long execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: lower tunnel lurks during A executes produce a kill or empty site ~38% of the time on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 lurk: CT mid flank through catwalk
knowledge.description: As the CT lurker on Dust2, hold cat during a B retake. When your team pushes B through B doors, push catwalk into mid to cut off the T escape through mid doors. Time your cat push to arrive 3 seconds after the B retake begins — Ts saving through mid will run into your angle at the top of mid.
knowledge.situation: CT-side Dust2, flanking during B retake
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: cat flanks during B retakes catch rotating Ts ~32% of the time on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 clutch: 1v1 CT defuse on A site from short corner
knowledge.description: In a 1v1 CT defuse situation on Dust2 A, use the short stairs corner as your anchor. Tap the defuse to force the T to peek from goose, elevator, or long, then hold the short angle. The stairs corner gives you a tight profile and forces the T into a wide swing. If no peek comes within 4 seconds, commit to a smoke defuse by throwing the smoke on the bomb from short.
knowledge.situation: 1v1 CT clutch on Dust2 A site
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: short corner 1v1 defuse attempts succeed ~44% of the time on Dust2 A

knowledge.title: Dust2 clutch: 1v3 T bomb timer play on B site
knowledge.description: In a 1v3 T post-plant on Dust2 B, plant for upper tunnel and retreat into the tunnel entrance. From tunnel you control the only bomb line of sight without exposing yourself to multiple angles. Molotov the bomb at 10 seconds to deny the defuse. The three CTs must push through the narrow B doors or window — pick them as they funnel through one at a time.
knowledge.situation: 1v3 T post-plant clutch on Dust2 B site
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tunnel post-plant with B plant wins ~22% of 1v3 situations on Dust2 B

knowledge.title: Dust2 aim: long doors to pit duel spot
knowledge.description: Hold the long A pit angle from the corner head-glitch position. This gives you a head-level angle on anyone pushing through long doors. Pre-aim the center of the doors gap — Ts peek in a predictable horizontal line. Counter-strafe and tap-fire at this range for maximum accuracy. The pit head-glitch exposes only your forehead and wins trades against Ts who have to peek out of the dark doors.
knowledge.situation: CT-side Dust2, holding long A from pit
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: pit head-glitch wins the long doors duel ~58% of the time at pro level on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 aim: mid doors wallbang through the gap
knowledge.description: The mid doors on Dust2 have a thin gap that allows wallbang shots. From T spawn, pre-aim the right edge of the doors gap at head height and fire 2-3 AK rounds during the CT crossing window at round start. The crossing window is between 3 and 5 seconds. This wallbang scores headshot kills in ~8% of rounds but costs nothing to attempt and can win the opening pick.
knowledge.situation: T-side Dust2, mid doors wallbang at round start
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: mid doors wallbang kills at round start occur in ~8% of rounds on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 support: A long execute triple-utility combo
knowledge.description: As the support player during a Dust2 A long execute, throw the A cross smoke (blocking the CT crossing from short to A site), the site molotov (clearing the A platform angle), and the car flash (blinding the car/elevator player). Execute in this order: cross smoke first to isolate the site, molotov second to flush the platform, flash last to cover the long swing. This 6-second combo reduces A defenders to the short player alone.
knowledge.situation: T-side Dust2, supporting A long execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: full cross-platform-car utility combo raises A long execute success to ~62% on Dust2

knowledge.title: Dust2 entry: alternate B entry through window boost
knowledge.description: Instead of the standard upper tunnel rush, boost a player through the B window for an alternate entry angle. The window entry bypasses the tunnel crossfire entirely and puts you above the site with a downward angle on the B platform defender. Pop flash through the window before dropping — this blinds the site player and the window drop lands behind the headshot box. Swing immediately after landing.
knowledge.situation: T-side Dust2, alternate B entry through window
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: window boost entries score first kill ~40% of the time on Dust2 B

knowledge.title: Dust2 economy: mid rush force-buy with Deagles
knowledge.description: On Dust2 eco rounds as T, buy three Desert Eagles and rush mid doors with a smoke and flash. Smoke the Xbox area, flash mid, and push through to cat or B. The Deagle one-shot headshot at mid range makes this viable against CTs crossing or holding mid. Even if you lose the round, Deagle headshots onto full-buy CTs destroy the CT economy. Focus on headshots only — body shots are wasted investment.
knowledge.situation: T-side Dust2, eco round mid push
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: mid Deagle rushes on eco rounds average 1.4 kills and break the CT economy ~22% of the time on Dust2
